projects.name: PomoRobo
projects.description: Do you ever wish you owned a Pixar Lamp that could help you study? PomoRobo is the next best thing! As part of a group of engineers at HackED 2026, I helped design and test PomoRobo, a modular assistive robotic arm that helps you stay focused and productive. I was tasked with designing the control logic and interfacing the electrical components of the arm. PomoRobo is comprised of an ELEGOO arduino, a stepper motor, a LCD display, and servo motors.
highlights: Built to 3 DOF to reduce complexity and improve usability.

projects.name: Domain Detector
projects.description: Collaborated on an AI model that recognizes the hand-signs from the anime Jujutsu Kaisen. Using a custom-trained TensorFlow model and MediaPipe’s hand-tracking, the system tracks 21 skeletal landmarks in real-time to classify which specific hand sign is being used. This project involved creating a custom neural network from scratch, handling complex dependency management in a Linux environment, and training a lightweight model capable of real-time detection without requiring a dedicated GPU.
highlights: Created a custom process to record hand movements
Reached a 95%+ success rate in identifying the correct gestures in real-time

projects.name: Prompty
projects.description: As a team of 4 university students ranging from various programs, we were selected as one of the top 12% applicants to propose a strategic solution aimed to mitigate student career anxiety through the use of a gamified platform that targets the users ability to think critically and make decisions ethically. I was tasked with assisting in the devleopment of the first iteration of the websites's frontend and presenting the final protoype to industry professionals. The website uses generated prompts that asks the user a question and encourages original and imaginative thinking.
highlights: Built for the Digital Innovation Case Competition Competition (DICC) 2026.

projects.name: Ted's Clanker
projects.description: Developed using Discord API, Flask, and a simple Python script, Ted's Clanker is a personal automated Discord bot that tracks user locations and informs members within a server when the user has left or arrived home safely. Once past 10:00 pm and the user has not returned home, Ted's Clanker informs the server and encourages others to check on the user. Using custom API endpoints, Ted's Clanker also allows for timed messages to be sent to the server which can be used as reminders for events or tasks.
highlights: Implemented custom API endpoints for real-time management of users location, reducing manual configuration time by 30%.
Deployed to Render Cloud hosting ensuring 99% uptime and continuous 24/7 operation.

projects.name: Fluffy dice design
projects.description: 3D modelled a fluffy dice design in SOLIDWORKS that can easily be hung on a keychain or car mirror!

projects.name: This Portfolio!
projects.description: Developed using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, this portfolio is a showcase of my projects and achievements. It is hosted on GitHub Pages and is deployed using Vercel. The portfolio is responsive and mobile-friendly, and is built with a modern and clean design.

experiences.title: Electrical Transmission Team Member
experiences.subtitle: Alberta Bionix | Edmonton, AB
experiences.details: Contributing to the development of an EMG-powered prosthetic leg, integrating gait-cycle-based EMG control with PID encoder feedback and motor control. As part of the electrical transmission team, I take ownership in designing the control system for the leg, utilizing an abundance of sensors, motor controllers, and the motor itself.

experiences.details: Co-founded Dr. E.P Scarlett’s first ever STEM club to inspire, promote, and encourage students to participate in science/engineering related activities and challenges regardless of experience and skill level.
